content.problemStatement:
There are NNN points on the number line, labeled 111 to NNN from left to right.

Chef is initially standing at point KKK.

Chef's goal is to reach point NNN. To do this, he can perform the following types of moves:


Move one step left - that is, if he is currently at point XXX, he can move to point Xâˆ’1X-1Xâˆ’1.
Move one step right - that is, if he is currently at point XXX, he can move to point X+1X+1X+1.
Mirror his position about the segment [1,N][1, N][1,N].

This means, if he is currently at point XXX, he will instantly move to point N+1âˆ’XN+1-XN+1âˆ’X.
The three types of moves can each be performed however many times he likes, and in any order.

Chef's position must lie in [1,N][1, N][1,N] at all times.

Find the **minimum** number of moves needed for Chef to reach point NNN, starting at KKK.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of input will contain two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of moves Chef needs to reach point NNN.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef starts at K=6K = 6K=6 and wants to reach position N=10N = 10N=10.
The fastest way is to just move one step to the right four times.
Test case 222: Chef starts at K=3K = 3K=3 and wants to reach position 101010.
This can be done in three moves, one method is as follows:

Move one step left. Chef's position is now 222.
Mirror his position. Chef's position is now Nâˆ’2+1=10âˆ’2+1=9N-2+1 = 10-2+1 = 9Nâˆ’2+1=10âˆ’2+1=9.
Move one step right. Chef's position is now 101010.

Test case 333: Chef is at position 111.
If he mirrors his position, he will immediately end up at position NNN. So, the answer is 111.
Test case 444: N=KN = KN=K, so Chef is already at his target position. No moves are needed.
